summary: Build M3 appointment, bulk action, and reason read-model UI
acceptance_criteria: Client read models expose M3 character, office, polity/vassal, obligation, vacancy/succession, appointment eligibility, bulk-action preview, and reason summaries without storing full WorldState in Redux/client store.

UI supports appointment and bulk appointment workflows, including per-target validation/rejection reasons, policy continuity visibility, and clear command submission through existing Worker/protocol paths.
Visible reason summaries are derived from protocol/query outputs and do not copy sim-core formulas or hidden full weights into React/UI code.
The interface shows why character behavior, appointment suitability, or obligation/proposal outcomes differ enough for M3 H-005 initial validation.
Storybook or Playwright coverage exercises appointment, bulk action, rejection reason, vacancy/succession, and obligation/reason display paths.
No authoritative domain state mutation, Pixi DisplayObject ownership by React, Electron/Node integration, full diplomacy UI, formal historical content, or new production dependency is introduced.
required_tests: Storybook story or Playwright test covers M3 appointment, bulk action, per-item rejection reason, and visible character/obligation reason flows.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-CLIENT-APPOINTMENT-UI-001
notes: This task covers the M3 appointment/bulk/reason UI/read-model requirement only.
If the UI needs a missing protocol query or reason code, route back to the relevant simulation task rather than duplicating rules client-side.
Keep React/Pixi boundaries unchanged; this task is DOM/read-model UI, not map renderer work.
